title: The Ruy Lopez
summary: The oldest serious opening in chess

steps.title: The Spanish
steps.text: Named after a 16th-century Spanish priest, the Ruy Lopez (or "Spanish") pressures the e5 pawn via the c6 knight. It leads to rich, nuanced middlegames. Every world champion from Steinitz to Carlsen has played it.

steps.hint: Pins the knight indirectly, threatens to undermine e5.

steps.title: Main Ideas
steps.text: The bishop doesn't actually win e5 immediately (after 3...a6 4.Bxc6 dxc6, Black's e-pawn is still defended). The REAL threat is to disrupt Black's structure and squeeze him positionally. Classical chess at its finest.
